title: I release the need to force sleep
subtitle: A three-minute release-and-allow for the effort of trying too hard to fall asleep.
excerpt: A three-minute pair of energy statements for the trying-to-force-sleep pattern. The effort of trying to fall asleep is one of the most reliable ways to prevent it — the body registers the effort as alertness. This practice releases the effort and allows the body to rest without the pressure of making sleep happen.
sourceNotes: Release / Allow structure: The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024), chapters 2 through 5, applied to sleep. The trying-to-force-sleep pattern is addressed in Day 2 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The combination as a bedtime reset is original to the homemade.education library.

practice.whenToUse: Use lying in bed when the effort of trying to sleep is what is keeping you awake. Also use before the lights go out if the anticipatory anxiety about the night has already started.

content.text: A three-minute pair of energy statements for the trying-to-force-sleep pattern. Trying to fall asleep is one of the most reliable ways to prevent it — the effort registers as alertness in the nervous system, which keeps the body awake. This practice releases the effort and allows the body to rest without the pressure of making sleep happen.

attrs.quote: I am ready to release the need to force sleep to come. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow my body to rest in its own time, without pressure or effort. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

content.text: Say each three times, slow, in your mind or quietly aloud. Lying down is fine; seated is fine.
If the trying is physical, muscle tension rather than mental effort, tap first.
Tapping to let the muscles melt
addresses the body's grip directly. Return to this release-and-allow after the tapping has softened the tension.

The release-and-allow structure is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024), chapters 2 through 5, applied here to sleep. The trying-to-force-sleep pattern is addressed in Day 2 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The combination as a bedtime reset is original to the homemade.education library.
